sourceRows.Title: Particle Models and Noncommutative Geometry
sourceRows.Summary: Original Connes-Lott finite-geometry framing of particle models as noncommutative gauge theory over an ordinary space plus a finite internal algebra.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Geometric mechanism lead; it does not supply GU-local source-lineage rows or observed W/Z/H pole masses.

sourceRows.Title: Noncommutative geometry and Higgs mechanism in the Standard Model
sourceRows.Summary: Uses a generalized field strength on a noncommutative/discrete extension of spacetime to obtain a Higgs potential and symmetry breaking.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Historical geometric-Higgs lead; any numerical relations depend on model choices and later obstructions/renormalization analysis.

sourceRows.Title: Higgs Mass and Noncommutative Geometry
sourceRows.Summary: Shows that electroweak NCG over continuous plus discrete space gives no forced special Higgs-mass relation once a gauge-invariant discrete curvature term is allowed.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Directly blocks promotion of simple finite-NCG Higgs/W mass ratios as target-independent predictions.

sourceRows.Title: Renormalization Group Analysis of the Higgs Boson Mass in a Noncommutative Geometry
sourceRows.Summary: Audits NCG initial conditions such as 4 lambda = g^2 = (5/3) g'^2, giving m_H = sqrt(2) m_W at the boundary and high low-energy Higgs masses after RG flow.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Numerical relation lead; it depends on a boundary scale, top mass, RG scheme, and does not match the observed 125 GeV Higgs without extra structure.

sourceRows.Title: Gravity and the Standard Model with neutrino mixing
sourceRows.Summary: Spectral-action finite-geometry model predicting high-scale couplings, Higgs scattering parameter, Yukawa sum, and a low-energy Higgs mass around 170 GeV.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Geometric unification lead already bounded by Phase268; original Higgs value is not observed and needs RG/Yukawa/cutoff inputs.

sourceRows.Title: Resilience of the Spectral Standard Model
sourceRows.Summary: Post-LHC repair route where an additional real scalar coupled to the Higgs changes RG behavior and restores compatibility with low Higgs mass.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Compatibility repair, not a GU-local prediction; it introduces extra scalar dynamics and high-scale boundary inputs.

sourceRows.Title: Higgs mass in Noncommutative Geometry
sourceRows.Summary: Reviews how an extra scalar generated from the Majorana neutrino mass can make the NCG Higgs computation compatible with the 126 GeV value.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Requires neutrino/Majorana and extra-scalar assumptions; does not provide W/Z rows or a target-independent GU Higgs scalar-source law.

sourceRows.Title: Noncommutative geometry, the Lorentzian Standard Model and its B-L extension
sourceRows.Summary: Studies 1-loop RG flow of Lorentzian Connes-Lott models and finds only the B-L extension compatible with top and Higgs masses in a small parameter region.
sourceRows.PromotionBoundary: Predictive external model but still depends on B-L extension, thresholds, neutrino matrix orientation, and high-scale parameter choices.

decision: Do not promote W/Z or Higgs physical masses from finite noncommutative-geometry or Connes-Lott discrete-Higgs routes in this repository. The sources supply a genuine geometric Higgs-as-discrete-connection mechanism and historical mass-relation leads, but simple Higgs/W relations are not invariant once all allowed discrete-geometry terms are included, spectral-action relations are high-scale/RG/Yukawa/cutoff dependent, and post-LHC compatibility requires extra scalar or B-L extension structure. Current GU artifacts still do not supply a finite-NCG map from Shiab/observer geometry, W/Z source rows, weak-angle/coupling source, observed photon/W/Z/H projection, target-independent VEV or mass scale, Higgs scalar-source/self-coupling lineage, RG/threshold transport, pole extraction, or GeV normalization.
nextRequiredArtifact: A GU-local derivation of an almost-commutative or finite internal algebra from Shiab/observer-sector geometry rather than importing an external spectral triple.
A GU Dirac-operator/algebra source that produces observed electroweak SU(2) x U(1), photon/W/Z projection, and separate W/Z source rows.
A target-independent Higgs scalar-source and self-coupling law that survives the Sitarz discrete-curvature obstruction and does not rely on fitted extra-scalar or B-L parameters.
A low-energy RG/threshold and physical-pole extractor with GeV normalization before any finite-NCG route can promote W/Z/H masses.
